titleEn: Niqo Robotics Charts Path to Profitability as Physical AI Weeding Platform Expands into New Crops, Markets, and New Generation of Products
titleZh: Niqo Robotics宣布盈利路径：物理AI除草平台扩展至新作物、新市场和新产品线
summaryZh: Niqo Robotics宣布其核心业务在首个完整商业年有望实现盈利，成为农业机器人领域首家公开达到这一里程碑的公司。其AI除草平台RoboWeeder基于计算机视觉实现毫米级精准除草，采用一次性购买、无订阅费的商业模式，已在加州、亚利桑那等地获得商业成功。公司正将技术扩展至洋葱、番茄、西兰花等15+种作物，并进军美国太平洋西北地区、欧洲和澳大利亚市场，同时计划于2026年下半年推出下一代除草机器人。
translationZh:
Niqo Robotics，这家为全球农场打造智能除草机器人的物理AI公司，今日宣布其核心业务在首个完整商业年有望实现盈利，这是农业机器人公司中尚未有公开达成的里程碑。公司同时公布了将其AI除草平台扩展至新作物、新美国市场以及全新产品形态的计划，标志着这家快速扩张的企业进入下一增长阶段。

**农业机器人领域首创：盈利路径**
在一个领先企业累计筹集数亿美元却未公布明确盈利时间表的行业中，Niqo Robotics正开辟一条不同的道路。该公司的RoboWeeder是一款AI驱动的精准除草系统，利用专有计算机视觉以毫米级精度识别和清除杂草，自进入市场以来已获得强劲的商业牵引力，核心业务正接近自给自足。公司资本高效的模式建立在“农民优先”的理念之上：一次性购买、零经常性费用、全天候服务以及本地备件库存。这一方式在Niqo已建立市场的加州萨利纳斯谷和亚利桑那尤马地区的种植者中引起了共鸣。

“我们创建Niqo是为了证明物理AI可以是一门真正的生意，而不仅仅是令人印象深刻却无限烧钱的技术，”Niqo Robotics创始人兼首席执行官Jaisimha Rao表示。“我们的盈利路径并非偷工减料。它是从第一天起就为种植者提供真正投资回报率的产品的结果。没有订阅费，没有隐藏成本，只有一台能自我回本的机器。”

**超越生菜：新作物、新市场、新形态**
随着核心生菜除草业务在商业上得到验证，Niqo正将其物理AI平台扩展到多个新前沿领域。

**新作物**：Niqo的AI除草技术正快速扩展到洋葱、番茄、西兰花、羽衣甘蓝、甜瓜和草坪草等特种作物。公司作物算法库将涵盖15种以上作物。

**新市场**：除了在加州、亚利桑那州和佐治亚州的现有业务，Niqo正进入太平洋西北地区，瞄准特种作物市场。公司还计划在未来一年内拓展至欧洲和澳大利亚的特种作物市场。

**新产品线**：Niqo宣布，针对美国市场的下一代精准除草机器人正在开发中，预计于2026年下半年推出。这款新产品将补充公司现有的RoboWeeder系列。
featuredReason: Niqo Robotics的盈利路径和跨作物扩展模式为智慧畜牧和精准农业领域提供了可复制的商业案例，其物理AI技术对养殖业中杂草管理、精准作业同样具有启发意义。
contentFull: By Niqo Robotics Share Niqo Robotics, the Physical AI company building intelligent weeding robots for the world’s farms, today announced that its core business is on track for profitability in its first full commercial year, a milestone no agricultural robotics company has publicly achieved. The company simultaneously unveiled plans to expand its AI weeding platform into new crops, new U.S. markets, and an entirely new product form factor, signaling the next phase of growth for the rapidly scaling enterprise. A First for Agricultural Robotics: Path to Profitability In an industry where leading players have collectively raised hundreds of millions of dollars without announcing a clear timeline to profitability, Niqo Robotics is charting a different course. The company’s RoboWeeder, an AI-powered precision weeding system that uses proprietary computer vision to identify and eliminate weeds at millimeter accuracy, has achieved strong commercial traction since its market entry, with the core business now approaching self-sustainability. The company’s capital-efficient model is built on a farmer-first philosophy: a one-time purchase with zero recurring fees, 24/7 service, and locally stocked parts. This approach has resonated with growers across California’s Salinas Valley and Arizona’s Yuma region, Niqo’s established markets. “We built Niqo to prove that Physical AI can be a real business, not just impressive technology that burns cash indefinitely,” said Jaisimha Rao, Founder and CEO of Niqo Robotics. “Our path to profitability is not about cutting corners. It is the result of building a product that delivers real ROI to growers from day one. No subscriptions, no hidden costs, just a machine that pays for itself.” MORE BY NIQO ROBOTICS Robotics | Automation June 19, 2026 Niqo Robotics Takes India-Built Physical AI Farming Platform to Europe at Bharat Innovates 2026 By Niqo Robotics|June 19, 2026 Expanding Beyond Lettuce: New Crops, New Markets, New Form Factors With its core lettuce weeding operations commercially proven, Niqo is now expanding its Physical AI platform across multiple new frontiers. Top Articles Read More Read More Read More Read More Read More Beyond Disruption: Earning Trust and Driving Impact in AgTech Through Grower-Led Innovation New Crops: Niqo’s AI weeding technology is expanding rapidly into specialty crops like onions, tomatoes, broccoli, kale, melons, and turf grass. The company will now have 15+ crops as a part of its crop algorithm library. New Markets: Beyond its established operations in California, Arizona and Georgia, Niqo is entering the Pacific Northwest region targeting the specialty crop market. It also targets expansion into Europe and the Australian specialty crop market in the upcoming year. New Product line: Niqo announced that a next-generation precision weeding robot is in development for the U.S. market, expected in the second half of 2026. This new product will complement the company’s existing RoboWeeder and dramatically expand the range of crops and field configurations the platform can serve. Physical AI: From Single Product to Intelligent Farming Platform At the core of Niqo’s expansion is Niqo Sense, a proprietary AI camera system that can be integrated into multiple form factors and retrofitted onto existing farm equipment. Niqo’s platform combines the power of AI on the edge along with a dual tank twin nozzle architecture system to perform weeding, thinning, and beneficial spraying in a single pass. This eliminates the need for multiple machines and multiple field operations. The system operates entirely on the edge, processing thousands of plant-level decisions per second in real-time, with zero cloud dependency, delivering over 99% accuracy even in weedy, challenging field conditions. This Physical AI approach, where intelligent software meets purpose-built hardware deployed in the real world, positions Niqo at the forefront of a category attracting significant investor attention globally. 0 1 5 Niqo Robotics Charts Path to Profitability as Physical AI Weeding Platform Expands into New Crops, Markets, and New Generation of Products Subscribe Today For Subscribe Niqo Robotics is a Physical AI company building intelligent agricultural robots for sustainable farming worldwide. Founded by Jaisimha Rao, a Carnegie Mellon-trained engineer and former BlackRock professional, Niqo develops AI-powered weeding, thinning, and precision spraying systems that help growers reduce costs, cut chemical usage, and overcome labor challenges. author: Matt Hopkins
contentHtml:
With its core weeding business approaching self-sustainability, the agricultural robotics company announces expansion into new crops and new geographies, alongside a next-generation weeding robot arriving later this year.
The post Niqo Robotics Charts Path to Profitability as Physical AI Weeding Platform Expands into New Crops, Markets, and New Generation of Products appeared first on Global Ag Tech Initiative.
